title: 0xD - The CPU
excerpt: Building the NES emulator CPU core: registers, flags, fetch-decode-execute, addressing modes, stack behavior, branches, and opcode execution.

title: 0xC - The Bus
excerpt: Wiring the emulator components together with the NES CPU memory map, RAM mirroring, PPU register mirrors, and cartridge routing.

title: 0xB - Parsing the Cartridge
excerpt: Parsing iNES cartridge files: headers, PRG-ROM, CHR-ROM, mapper flags, trainer handling, and mirroring metadata for the emulator.

title: 0xA - Understanding 6502 Assembly
excerpt: Part one of the NES emulator series, covering 6502 registers, memory, instructions, addressing basics, flags, branches, and the stack.

title: Integrating MISP with Wazuh
excerpt: Integrating MISP threat intelligence with Wazuh SIEM to automatically detect known malware and enrich security alerts with context.

title: Reclaiming Clean Syscalls in Userland
excerpt: EDRs hook Nt* functions with jmp stubs, walk the EAT to detect them, reclaim clean syscalls by loading a fresh ntdll from disk and inline stub patching.

title: Generic Syscall Stub
excerpt: Instead of writing a separate stub per syscall, one generic stub that handles every single Nt* function, continuation of Syscalls Demystified.

title: Syscalls Demystified - Direct, Indirect, and Dynamic SSN Resolution
excerpt: How syscalls work from usermode to kernel, and how to invoke them directly, indirectly, and with dynamic SSN resolution to bypass EDR hooks.

title: Hiding Imports with PEB and EAT Walking
excerpt: Walking the PEB to find loaded DLL bases and manually parsing the EAT to resolve function addresses without touching LoadLibrary or GetProcAddress.

title: Queue Early Bird Injection
excerpt: Early Bird APC injection, queuing shellcode to a suspended thread.

title: Process Hollowing and Base Relocations
excerpt: A deep dive into process hollowing covering entry-point shellcode overwrite and full PE replacement with relocation handling.

title: Pwnable.tw - Start
excerpt: Writeup for Pwnable.tw Start, covering the stack leak, ret-to-write trick, and shellcode injection path to code execution.

title: Pwnable.tw - orw
excerpt: Writeup for Pwnable.tw orw, focused on the seccomp constraint and building shellcode that uses only open, read, and write.

title: Heap Exploitation Techniques
excerpt: Some of Heap Exploitation Refreshers and Techniques.

title: Binary Exploitation
excerpt: Notes covering classic pwn concepts from command injection and stack abuse to ret2libc, heap bugs, and modern binary exploitation primitives.

title: Memory Forensics - MemLabs
excerpt: A long-form MemLabs walkthrough focused on Volatility workflows, RAM triage, and practical digital forensics techniques.
